title: Flaky Fried Venezuelan Peas Empanadas
description: Savory Venezuelan empanadas filled with frozen peas and wrapped in a flaky dough, perfect for a comforting snack or meal. This dish is kosher-friendly and features a mortar-pestle-pound filling.
instructions: In a mortar, pound the thawed peas with a pestle until coarsely chopped.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cold butter to the bowl and use a pastry blender or fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the water to the bowl, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place 1 tablespoon of the chopped pea mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Fry the empanadas until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
